name: Who are you creating this notification for?
pageTitle: Who are you creating this notification for - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: About the consignment
heading: Who are you creating this notification for?

doaObservations.finding: This delegated-agent-only page is now CONFIRMED rendered by five further DoA traces beyond the CSV trace the base spec cited (9a918b86). All render the same two-radio fork (own org labelled with the agent's own organisation name, plus 'A different organisation') under caption 'About the consignment'.
traces: e9b5e36b926ee3b7ac855c45cf8169dd38321ad4 snap 11 (action 11) — radios 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C' + 'A different organisation'; 'A different organisation' checked (action 10)
b1f31ed70fbdb2f92f66488da5bda6f3e510a0c9 action 11 (before-snapshot) — 'A different organisation' checked at action 10
b701cc405623fb2d2e2b2dedcf2bec3d10dfb954 snap 11 (before@call@361) — same two radios, 'A different organisation' selected
5dd87b7fee11881f5706c26e17617e68b19247d1 action 11 (before-snapshot) — OWN-ORG branch: 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C' radio CHECKED (value=true)

doaObservations.finding: UPGRADE — the own-organisation branch (for-own-organisation=true, 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C') is now CONFIRMED rendered AND selected, not merely inferred from the handler + one test as the base spec's field note states. Trace 5dd87b7f is the own-org submission (Carol Clark submits for her own org Plant Agency C); the own-org radio is checked at action 10 and the resulting notification carries NO Trade Partner badge and is invisible to other-org members — see authorization-rules.md.

ACCESS-CONTROL CONFIRMATION — the choice made here is a permissions boundary, now proven by paired rendered traces rather than only the spec grouping. Selecting a different (delegated) org makes the notification visible to that org's members (065de8c5, 2c16ad65, e9b5e36b); selecting own org keeps it private to the agent's own org (5dd87b7f). Full rule set in authorization-rules.md.

TEST-SIDE ABSENCE RE-CONFIRMED AND STRENGTHENED this pass: a grep for overdue|debt across ipaffs-qa-automation/{config,tests,page-objects,workflows} returns ZERO hits, and config/users.ts:1-114 cannot express debt state even in principle — it declares a fixed USER_ROLES tuple (:1-14) and sources only userId/password from env vars (:25-45). Organisation identity and debt status live in the B2C/backend account behind those credentials, not in any repo fixture, so no test could opt into this branch without a new seeded account.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: <legend class="govuk-fieldset__legend"></legend> — empty; <h1> rendered OUTSIDE the fieldset
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The fieldset wrapping the two radios has a legend element containing only whitespace. The page H1 ('Who are you creating this notification for?') sits above and outside the fieldset, emitted by the common/pageTitle partial. The shared govuk/radios partial IS invoked without legendText or legendIsPageHeading, so its legend renders empty.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: This is a real accessibility defect, not just a style nit. The GOV.UK radios pattern for a single-question page puts the H1 inside the legend (legend class govuk-fieldset__legend--xl > h1 class govuk-fieldset__heading), so the question is announced as the group's name. Here the radio group has NO accessible name — the Playwright a11y tree shows a bare 'group' with no name. A screen-reader user hears the two options with no question attached. IMPORTANT — the cause is a CALLER CHOICE, not a structural limitation: the shared govuk/radios partial already supports legendText and legendIsPageHeading and will emit legend > h1.govuk-fieldset__heading when given them (radios.html:3-7). consignmentFor.html:23-28 simply passes neither, and calls common/pageTitle separately instead. So the fix in IPAFFS would be to pass the legend params and drop the pageTitle call for this page — no partial rework needed. The new app should render the legend/H1 together per the standard pattern. NOTE: this page is NOT covered by the CHED-PP accessibility suite — that spec authenticates as b2cNotifier (a non-delegated user) and therefore steps straight from Import Type to Country of Origin, never rendering this page. So the defect has never been axe-tested. WHY IT SURVIVED: the QA page object resolves both radios by accessible NAME (getByRole('radio', { name: ... }), OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:6-8,:18-20). Per-item labels are correctly named, so the functional tests pass regardless of the unnamed group — the defect is invisible to the only suite that touches the page.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Standard GOV.UK Radios with legend-as-page-heading: fieldset > legend.govuk-fieldset__legend--xl > h1.govuk-fieldset__heading, with the caption inside the legend above the h1.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 22 (main.html: <legend class="govuk-fieldset__legend "> containing only whitespace; a11y tree 'group [ref=e61]' with no name); legend emitted by ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/partials/govuk/radios.html:3-7, which supports legendIsPageHeading/legendText at :4-6; caller omits them at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/consignmentFor.html:23-28; H1 emitted separately by common/pageTitle, invoked at consignmentFor.html:14 and defined at partials/common/pageTitle.html:4; a11y coverage gap corroborated at ipaffs-qa-automation/tests/accessibility/ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:16 (test.use storageState b2cNotifier) and :20-33 (dashboard -> Import Type -> Country of Origin, no organisationSelection step)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: div.navigation-links / div.navigation-links__left-block wrapping the back link, a '|' divider and a govuk-breadcrumbs trail; back link href="#"
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: IPAFFS renders its own navigation strip above main, combining the GOV.UK back link with a div.navigation-links__divider '|' and a single-item govuk-breadcrumbs trail ('Dashboard'). Both the back link and the breadcrumbs carry extra custom modifier classes (back-link--next-to-breadcrumbs, breadcrumbs--next-to-back) that exist purely to make them sit side by side. The back link's href is '#', so navigation depends on client-side JS. The same strip also contains an information-banner with the signed-in user's name and Address book / Manage account / Manage trade partners / Sign out links.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom BEM classes outside the Design System, a Design System component (Breadcrumbs) relocated out of its intended beforeContent slot and restyled to sit inline with the back link, and a back link that is not a real link — it will not work without JS and cannot be opened in a new tab or used by keyboard/AT as a normal link. GOV.UK guidance is that the back link should point at the actual previous page URL and progressive enhancement should not be required for basic navigation; back link and breadcrumbs are also not intended to be combined on the same page. The new app should emit a plain govuk-back-link with a server-known href and drop the bespoke nav strip.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-back-link with a real href, placed in the beforeContent block of the GOV.UK page template. Pick back link OR breadcrumbs, not both.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 22 (div.navigation-links > div.navigation-links__left-block > a#back-link.govuk-back-link.back-link--next-to-breadcrumbs[href='#'] + div.navigation-links__divider '|' + div.govuk-breadcrumbs.breadcrumbs--next-to-back; a11y tree link "Back" /url: "#" ref=e48, list ref=e51); ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/consignmentFor.html:2-8. No QA test drives the back link on this page (OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:1-42 exposes no back-link locator), so its JS behaviour is untested here.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Own-organisation radio label is dynamic user data, yet hardcoded as a literal string in the QA page object
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The first radio's label is the signed-in user's companyDisplayName. The page object pins it to the literal 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C' (the test agent's org) rather than parameterising it, even though a parameterised radioCompany(companyName) locator exists two lines above and is used for the delegated-org list on the NEXT page.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not an app defect — a test-suite fragility worth recording because it tells you the label really is per-user data, not fixed copy. Any rebuild that renames the test org breaks this locator. It also means the tests only ever prove the own-org branch for ONE org name. CORROBORATED this pass: 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C' appears nowhere in config/ — a case-insensitive grep for 'agency c' across config/, page-objects/ and tests/ hits only OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:19 and the doa spec titles/assertions. The org name is not a declared fixture anywhere; it is a property of the live B2C account behind B2C_DOA_AGENT_USER_ID, restated as a literal in the page object. So the test suite has no single source of truth for it.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: n/a — this is a test-design observation, not a Design System deviation.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/notification/OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:18-20 (hardcoded 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C') vs :14-16 (parameterised radioCompany); value source at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_for.js:72-73,78 (loggedInUser.userInfo.companyDisplayName); absence from fixtures confirmed by grep -rni 'agency c' over ipaffs-qa-automation/{config,page-objects,tests} (only OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:19 + doa-agent-access.spec.ts:60,66 + doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:65,92,102,109) and by config/users.ts:1-45 (roles + env-sourced credentials only; no org names)

fields.name: for-own-organisation
fields.label: (no accessible name — the fieldset legend is empty; the visible question is the H1 outside the fieldset)
fields.mandatorinessNote: legacy — requiredness is a FRONTEND rule (Joi.boolean().required() at validation/.../consignment_for.js:15-17, error ON_BEHALF_OF_REQUIRED at en.js:158), NOT a PartOne.java @NotNull group. The for-own-organisation BOOLEAN itself is never persisted — it is a DoA routing input the frontend consumes to decide the owning org. But its OUTCOME is persisted: when the agent chooses 'a different organisation', the notification stores agencyOrganisationId — 'Organisation id which the agent user belongs to, stored against each notification which has been raised on behalf of another organisation' (notification-schema.json:977-979). That field is a plain, UN-annotated optional String on the top-level Notification model (Notification.java:68 — no @NotNull, not on PartOne, no CHED-PP validation group), so legacy mandatoriness on the model side is: the persisted consequence is OPTIONAL and only set on the different-org branch. The two prompt-highlighted legacy model/message sources therefore carry: PartOne.java — nothing (this is not a PartOne field); the microservice ValidationMessages.properties — nothing (grep for behalf/on-behalf/ownOrganisation returns zero). The user-facing required rule lives only in the frontend Joi. POLICY THE REBUILD MAY REVISIT: the 'required' here only makes sense while the two-radio own-vs-different split exists; the openQuestion proposing one combined org list (own org as a sibling radio among delegated orgs) would replace this boolean with a single 'Select an organisation' required field and give the group a real accessible name.
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: legacy — frontend rule at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_for.js:15-17 (Joi.boolean().required(), ON_BEHALF_OF_REQUIRED); persisted outcome agencyOrganisationId at ipaffs-imports-notification-schema/notification-schema-java/.../representation/Notification.java:68 (String, no @NotNull) and notification-schema-core/resources/notification-schema.json:977-979 (description); model/message silence confirmed by grep -rni 'onBehalfOf|ownOrganisation|forOwnOrganisation|behalf' over ipaffs-imports-notification-schema/ (only PartTwo.signedOnBehalfOf — a decision-signing field, unrelated) and grep 'behalf|on-behalf|ownOrganisation' over ipaffs-notification-microservice/.../ValidationMessages.properties (zero hits)
fields.observedValuesNote: value=false ('A different organisation') confirmed in traces 9a918b86, e9b5e36b, 253df9dc, b1f31ed7, b701cc40. value=true ('IPAFFS Plant Agency C' — the agent's own org) now CONFIRMED rendered and selected in DoA trace 5dd87b7fee11881f5706c26e17617e68b19247d1 action 10 (own-org submission); the base spec had this as inferred-only.
fields.conditionalOn: Whole page is conditional. GET only renders this page when type=CHEDPP AND the signed-in user has at least one delegation (customerService.getDelegations(...).length > 0). Otherwise it redirects straight to consignment-origin. So a non-DoA user never sees this page, and no other CHED type reaches it — hasDelegations is only evaluated when type === CHEDPP, even though the query validator accepts CVEDA, CVEDP, CED and CHEDPP. A missing type redirects to IMPORTER_CONSIGNMENT_PAGE_1. CORROBORATED BY THE TESTS: the CHED-PP workflow only visits this page when the caller passes organisationName or useOwnOrganisation (both default to falsy), and the two non-delegated journeys — the org1-member creation test and the whole accessibility suite — step from Import Type straight to Country of Origin with no org-selection step at all.
fields.evidence: trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 22 (radios rendered; action log shows locator resolved to input[type=radio][value=false][id=for-own-organisation-2][name=for-own-organisation], checked by the test); options + conditionality corroborated at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_for.js:33-43 (hasDelegations gate), :63-69 (query validator valid types), :77-80 (radioItems); test-side conditionality at ipaffs-qa-automation/workflows/notification/ched-pp-workflows.ts:259-264 (if organisationName -> selectDifferentOrganisation; else if useOwnOrganisation -> selectOwnOrganisation; else skip the page entirely — the comment at :259 reads 'Organisation Selection Page (DoA agents)') with defaults organisationName:'' and useOwnOrganisation:false at :136-137 and the interface fields at :43,:45; skip-path confirmed at ipaffs-qa-automation/tests/auth/DoA/doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:8-31 (org1 member, no org args) and ipaffs-qa-automation/tests/accessibility/ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:16-33. BOTH VALUES ARE DRIVEN BY TESTS even though only value=false was traced: value=false (a different organisation) via selectDifferentOrganisation at OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:36-41, used by doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:44-46,78-81,124-127, doa-agent-access.spec.ts:14-17,41-44 and doa-importer-access.spec.ts:14-17,41-45; value=true (own organisation) via selectOwnOrganisation at OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:26-29, reached ONLY from doa-agent-access.spec.ts:70-73 (useOwnOrganisation: true) — the sole test in the repo that sets that flag (grep -rn useOwnOrganisation over tests/ returns exactly one hit).

fields.label: (hidden — no visible label)

fields.name: crumb

fields.name: etag
fields.label: (hidden — optimistic concurrency token)
fields.conditionalOn: Rendered only when an etag is present in the view context ({{#if etag}}) — i.e. when editing an existing notification rather than creating a new one. Validation adds ETAG_SCHEMA only when validate() is called with requireEtag. The amend-from-review journey (Amend -> Change organisation -> Save and review) is the flow that would exercise this, and it IS covered by a test — but no trace captured the DOM, so the hidden input itself remains unobserved.
fields.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/consignmentFor.html:19-21; ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_for.js:26-31 (requireEtag branch); schema shape at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/common_schemas.js:45-51 (Joi.string().ruleset.min(16).max(18).rule(...).required()) — CONFIRMED ABSENT from the observed create-flow DOM (the form#consignment-for at action 22 contains only crumb, notification-type and the two radios, and no input[name=etag] exists anywhere in the document; note the document DOES carry further controls outside this form — the cookie banner's accept-cookies button and the footer cookie form's crumb/returnUrl/fromFooterHeader — so the 'only' claim is scoped to the page form, not the document); the amend flow that should render it is driven at ipaffs-qa-automation/tests/auth/DoA/doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:92-95 (btnAmend -> linkChangeOrganisation -> selectOwnOrganisationAndSaveAndReview). That test asserts only the resulting organisation name (:109), never a concurrency conflict, so the etag's actual PURPOSE — rejecting a stale edit — is untested at every level.

fields.name: save-and-continue / save-and-return / save-and-review
fields.label: (payload-only — accepted by the validator, never submitted by this page)
fields.conditionalOn: The POST validator spreads SAVE_ACTIONS_SCHEMA, which permits three OPTIONAL save-action button fields. But the buttons this page renders carry a value attribute and NO name attribute, so nothing is ever submitted under these keys from this page. They are accepted-but-unused here; other pages in the service render named save-action buttons.
fields.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_for.js:23 (...SAVE_ACTIONS_SCHEMA); definition at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/common_schemas.js:39-43 (SAVE_AND_CONTINUE_SCHEMA + SAVE_AND_RETURN_SCHEMA + SAVE_AND_REVIEW_SCHEMA, each Joi.string().valid(...).optional()); absence of a name attribute on the rendered button confirmed at trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada action 22 (full control inventory: BUTTON|submit||button-save-and-continue|Save and continue). Note the QA page object also distinguishes the two buttons only by label (getByRole('button', { name: 'Save and continue' | 'Save and review' }), OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:10-12,:22-24), consistent with there being no name attribute to key off.

validationMessages.message: Select who you are creating this notification for
validationMessages.trigger: Submitting with neither radio selected. for-own-organisation is Joi.boolean().required(); the ANY_REQUIRED error maps to ON_BEHALF_OF_REQUIRED, and the handler re-renders the view with errors rather than redirecting.
validationMessages.confidenceNote: COPY is legacy (verbatim from en.js:158, the single authoritative source) so the tag is 'legacy', not the pre-authorisation 'inferred'. What remains unverified from both admissible sources is the RENDERING (error-summary title, link behaviour, inline placement) — no trace captured this error state and no QA test submits the page empty (OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:1-42 has no error locator; all four methods check a radio first). Trust the string; not its presentation.
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:158 (verbatim: ON_BEHALF_OF_REQUIRED: 'Select who you are creating this notification for'); wired at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_for.js:15-17; re-render at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_for.js:50-54 — no trace captured this error state, AND no QA test asserts this message. RE-VERIFIED this pass: OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:1-42 has no error locator, and all four of its methods (:26-41) check a radio before clicking submit, so the empty-submit path is unreachable from the suite. Both admissible sources are silent on how it RENDERS. The string itself is verbatim from source.

validationMessages.trigger: Hidden notification-type input absent from the POST payload (any.required). NOT reachable through the UI — the template always renders the input, so this fires only on a tampered/replayed payload. Recorded for completeness of the POST contract, not as user-facing copy.
validationMessages.confidenceNote: COPY is legacy: fully determined by source (invalidEntryMessage helper 'Enter a valid ' + field.toLowerCase() at en.js:23-25, over label NOTIFICATION_TYPE 'Notification type' at labels/en.js:69). Upgraded from the pre-authorisation 'inferred'. Unreachable via UI (payload-tampering only), so never rendered — but the string itself is source-derivable, not guessed.
validationMessages.evidence: composed at runtime by invalidEntryMessage('Notification type') — helper at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:23-25 ('Enter a valid ' + field.toLowerCase()), label NOTIFICATION_TYPE: 'Notification type' at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/labels/en.js:69; wired at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_for.js:18-22 — never rendered in any trace; no QA test asserts it (the suite only ever drives the page through the UI)

validationMessages.message: Notification type must be a string
validationMessages.trigger: Hidden notification-type input present but not a string (string.base). Payload tampering only — unreachable via the UI.
validationMessages.confidenceNote: COPY is legacy: fully determined by source (typeMessage helper `${label} must be a ${type}` at en.js:27-29, label 'Notification type' at labels/en.js:69). Upgraded from 'inferred'. Payload-tampering only, so never rendered — string is source-derivable.

validationMessages.message: You cannot submit a CHEDPP import notification on behalf of {organisation names}.
validationMessages.trigger: Rendered inside the overdue-debt Details disclosure when the signed-in user's own organisation is an overdue debtor. Accompanied by: summary text 'Why some options are not available', a 'There is a problem' paragraph, and 'Please ask {organisation names} to contact us on 01633 631800 to discuss the reactivation of their account.' The org names are comma-separated via the commaSeparated helper over overdueDebtorOrgNames. On the review page the same partial instead renders a full govuk-error-summary titled 'There is a problem'; on THIS page it is the details variant. The word 'submit' becomes 'clone' in the cloning flow.
validationMessages.confidenceNote: COPY is legacy: transcribed verbatim from the template partial overdueDebtWarningDoA.html:24-25 (authoritative source). Upgraded from 'inferred'. What is unverified is BEHAVIOUR — no trace rendered it and the QA repo cannot reach an overdue-debtor account (grep overdue|debt across config/tests/page-objects/workflows returns zero; config/users.ts sources only role + env credentials, never debt state). Note the hardcoded phone number 01633 631800 is embedded in the template copy.
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/partials/importer/overdueDebt/overdueDebtWarningDoA.html:24-25 (verbatim copy), :12-16 (details summary 'Why some options are not available'), :20 ('There is a problem'), :1-9 (isReviewPage branch); overdueDebtorOrgNames supplied at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_for.js:82 — never rendered in any trace. QA-side silence re-confirmed this pass: grep for overdue|debt across ipaffs-qa-automation/{config,tests,page-objects,workflows} returns zero hits, and config/users.ts:1-45 exposes only role + env-sourced userId/password, so no fixture can select a debtor account. Copy is read from source, not observed.

openQuestions: No trace in the corpus captured an error state on this page, and no QA test submits it empty — the page object exposes no error-summary or inline-error locator (OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:1-42) and all four of its methods check a radio before submitting (:26-41). So the error-summary rendering (title text, link behaviour, inline message placement) is unverified from BOTH admissible sources. The message copy itself is verbatim from en.js:158 and is safe to trust; its presentation is not.

RESOLVED (was: unexercised 'Save and review' branch) — the template and the QA repo together show exactly when it appears and where it goes. The template branches on showSaveAndReviewButton (consignmentFor.html:34-40). Entry is the review screen's 'Change organisation' link (#organisation-change-link, ReviewNotificationPage.ts:125-127) reached after clicking Amend on a draft; the page then renders 'Save and review' instead of 'Save and continue' and returns the user straight back to the review screen (doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:92-99). So yes — an edit-from-CYA flow returns to check-answers, and the rebuild should keep that. Still unobserved in any trace, so the rendered DOM of the variant is inferred, not confirmed.

PARTLY RESOLVED (was: overdue-debt copy unknown) — the copy IS in source and is transcribed in validationMessages above; it was previously mis-recorded here as unknown. When the signed-in user's organisation is an overdue debtor, the own-organisation radio is rendered disabled with the per-item hint 'This option is not available' (consignment_for.js:78), and a govuk-details disclosure 'Why some options are not available' explains that the user cannot submit a CHEDPP notification on behalf of that org and must ring 01633 631800 (overdueDebtWarningDoA.html:12-25). What remains genuinely open is BEHAVIOUR, not copy: no trace ever rendered it and the QA repo cannot reach it — grep for overdue|debt across config/, tests/, page-objects/ and workflows/ returns zero hits, and config/users.ts (:1-45) declares a fixed role tuple with env-sourced credentials only, so debt status is a property of the live B2C/backend account, not anything a fixture can set. Testing this branch would need a purpose-seeded debtor account. This is a business rule (debtors may not create notifications for their own org, only on behalf of others) that the rebuild must consciously keep or drop — flagging it because it is easy to miss and it silently changes who can do what. Also note the hardcoded phone number in the template.

The first radio's label is dynamic — it is the signed-in user's companyDisplayName from the JWT userInfo (consignment_for.js:72-73), not fixed copy. 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C' is just this test account's org, and the page object hardcodes that literal (OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:19) rather than parameterising it. Corroborated this pass: the string appears nowhere in config/ — it is not a declared fixture at all, just a literal in the page object mirroring whatever org the B2C_DOA_AGENT account happens to belong to. The new app needs the same value from its identity source.

CORRECTION + firmed up — routing out of this page is value-dependent: true (own organisation) -> consignment-origin; false (a different organisation) -> consignment-organisation. Confirmed in the handler (consignment_for.js:56-60); only the false branch was traced. EVIDENCE UPGRADE this pass — the false branch's destination is not merely handler-inferred, it is CONFIRMED in the trace network log: request 893 is POST 302 consignment-for?type=CHEDPP, immediately followed by request 894 GET 200 consignment-organisation?type=CHEDPP (trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada). The true branch (-> consignment-origin) remains inferred from the handler alone — no trace in this corpus captured it. The previous revision of this spec cited doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:95 as driving the true branch via selectOwnOrganisation — that citation was WRONG: :95 calls selectOwnOrganisationAndSaveAndReview (the amend/save-and-review variant, which returns to the review screen and does NOT exercise the create-flow routing). The true branch through the normal create flow is driven by exactly ONE test in the repo — doa-agent-access.spec.ts:70-73 (useOwnOrganisation: true), the only hit for that flag across tests/ — via ched-pp-workflows.ts:262-263 -> OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:26-29. The false branch's destination is corroborated test-side: selectDifferentOrganisation checks the radio, submits, then checks a radio named after the delegated org and submits AGAIN (OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:36-41) — i.e. the next page is a second radio list, one option per delegated organisation.

NEW — this page is an ACCESS-CONTROL decision, not just a data field, and the tests say so explicitly. Choosing 'a different organisation' makes the notification visible to that organisation's members (doa-importer-access.spec.ts:7-32 and doa-agent-access.spec.ts:7-32: agent creates for Plant Org 1, Isabel Irwin of Plant Org 1 can view/copy/amend it); choosing 'own organisation' makes it INVISIBLE to them (doa-agent-access.spec.ts:60-86: agent creates for her own Plant Agency C, and Isabel's dashboard search for the reference asserts toBeHidden). The agent retains visibility either way (doa-agent-access.spec.ts:34-58). So the radio selected here permanently determines who else can see and amend the consignment. The rebuild must treat this as a permissions boundary with server-side enforcement — not merely a label on the record — and should have its own tests for the negative case. Flagging because nothing on the page itself tells the user this consequence: there is no hint, no body copy, no warning.

NEW — a second visibility rule interacts with this page and the rebuild should confirm it is intended: DRAFTS are not visible to the delegated organisation's members even when created FOR them. doa-importer-access.spec.ts:34-58 has the agent create a draft for Plant Org 1 (stopAtAccompanyingDocuments: true) and asserts Isabel cannot find it (:56); the same journey submitted IS visible (:7-32). So org members see a notification only once the agent submits it. Is that deliberate (agents own their work-in-progress) or an artefact? It matters because it means the org cannot review or intervene before submission.

The radio group has no accessible name because the legend is empty (see nonStandardPatterns). Worth confirming with the a11y/design team that this is a known IPAFFS defect and not something to carry forward. Note the accessibility suite never renders this page (it runs as a non-delegated user, ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:16), so nobody has ever axe-tested it — the defect is unflagged rather than accepted. It is also invisible to the functional tests, which resolve the radios by their per-item accessible names (getByRole('radio', { name })) and so pass regardless of the unnamed group. Note the shared radios partial already supports the correct legend-as-heading pattern, so in IPAFFS this is a one-line caller fix, which strengthens the case that it is an oversight rather than a deliberate decision.

DOWNSTREAM CONSEQUENCE the rebuild must decide on: the organisation chosen here is not just a label — it drives the notification's owning org and pre-populates later pages. Tests assert that (a) the notification is assigned to the chosen org (reviewNotification.locatorOrganisationName, doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:29,61,109), (b) the dashboard shows a 'Trade Partner' badge when an agent creates for a delegated org (:57-58), and (c) contact details AND importer name/address are auto-populated from the delegated organisation (:129-138 — contact name 'Isabel Irwin', her email and telephone, importer 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1' plus the full address 'KAINOS SOFTWARE LTD, 4-6, UPPER CRESCENT, BELFAST, BT7 1NT'). Is that auto-population a requirement of the new service, and does changing the org later re-derive those values or leave stale ones? The amend test changes org after those fields are set but asserts only the org name afterwards (:109), so the re-derivation behaviour is untested — and given the auto-populated contact/importer come from the OLD org, a stale-data bug here would go unnoticed by the current suite. Worth an explicit decision plus a test in the rebuild.

The two radios are semantically 'own org' vs 'someone else', but only the second leads to an actual org choice. Should the new app collapse this into ONE page listing the user's own org alongside each delegated org as sibling radio options? That would remove a page and give the radio group a real question to be named by. Raising it because the two-page split looks like an artefact of IPAFFS's boolean model (for-own-organisation is a Joi.boolean) rather than a user need. Note the QA page object already treats the two pages as one logical step — selectDifferentOrganisation() spans both (OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:36-41) — which is mild evidence that testers, at least, think of it as a single choice.

The page URL accepts type=CVEDA|CVEDP|CED|CHEDPP (query validator, consignment_for.js:63-69) but the POST validator only accepts notification-type=CHEDPP (validation/.../consignment_for.js:18-22), and the GET only renders for CHEDPP. So three of the four accepted query types can only ever redirect. Dead surface the rebuild should not reproduce — worth confirming that delegation genuinely is CHED-PP-only and not an unfinished rollout to the other CHED types. The QA repo is consistent with CHED-PP-only: the org-selection page object is referenced solely from ched-pp-workflows.ts and the three DoA specs (grep over tests/, workflows/, page-objects/), with no CHED-A/P/D equivalent.
